# Render request (prompt timesheet-v1) Module: timesheet-web Stack: python-lib ## Definitions - App factory: `create_web_app(store_path)` returning a FastAPI application bound to one store file. - Entries page: the HTML document served at `GET /` listing entries and hosting the filter toolbar and the add form. - Entry row: a `` carrying `data-testid="entry-row"` that shows one entry's id, project, date, hours, status badge, and actions. - Filter form: the GET `
` carrying `data-testid="filter-form"` and the class `ts-toolbar`, laying its `week` and `project` text inputs and its submit button out in one row. - Add form: the `` carrying `data-testid="add-form"` inside a `ts-card` that posts fields `project`, `date`, and `hours` to `POST /entries`. - Error page: an HTML response rendered through the ui-kit page shell whose body contains a `ts-error` element naming the problem. - Style lock: every HTML response is built with the ui-kit `page` shell, contains no `style=` attribute, and every `class` attribute names only `ts-`-prefixed kit classes. ## Implementation requirements - Use Python 3.12. - Expose `create_web_app(store_path)` from `src/timesheet_web/`. - Declare `fastapi`, `httpx`, and `python-multipart` in the generated `pyproject.toml` `[project]` dependencies. - Persist entries only through the required timestore module and enforce every rule only through the required rules module; the app holds no state of its own. - Build every HTML response — the entries page and every error page — by passing body markup to the required ui-kit module's `page` with the title `Timesheet`; never emit a `
response:

{html.escape(message)}

store.entries_for_week(week) return [entry for entry in entries if entry["project"] == project] if week: return store.entries_for_week(week) if project: return store.entries_for_project(project) return store.list_entries() def create_web_app(store_path) -> FastAPI: """Build a FastAPI app bound to the timesheet store at ``store_path``.""" app = FastAPI() store = TimeStore(store_path) @app.get("/", response_class=HTMLResponse) def list_entries(week: str | None = None, project: str | None = None) -> HTMLResponse: try: entries = _filtered_entries(store, week, project) except StoreError as exc: return _error_page(str(exc), 422) body = _filter_form(week, project) + _entries_body(entries) return HTMLResponse(page("Timesheet", body)) return app "}, {"path": "tests/test_web_filter.py", "action": "write", "root": "module", "contents": """"Unit tests for filtering the timesheet web entries page.""" from fastapi.testclient import TestClient from timesheet_web import create_web_app from timestore import TimeStore def test_filter_query_params_prefill_form(tmp_path): store_path = tmp_path / "store.json" store = TimeStore(store_path) store.add_entry("Apollo", "2026-07-06", 2.5) app = create_web_app(store_path) client = TestClient(app) response = client.get("/?week=2026-W28&project=Apollo") assert response.status_code == 200 assert 'value="2026-W28"' in response.text assert 'value="Apollo"' in response.text def test_malformed_week_returns_422(tmp_path): app = create_web_app(tmp_path / "store.json") client = TestClient(app) response = client.get("/?week=not-a-week") assert response.status_code == 422 assert "ts-error" in response.text "}, {"path": "FR2/test_fr2.py", "action": "write", "root": "conformance", "contents": """"Conformance tests for FR2: filter the page by week and project.""" import re from fastapi.testclient import TestClient from timesheet_web import create_web_app from timestore import TimeStore def _make_client(tmp_path): store_path = tmp_path / "store.json" app = create_web_app(store_path) return TestClient(app), store_path def test_filter_by_week_narrows_rows_and_total(tmp_path): store_path = tmp_path / "store.json" store = TimeStore(store_path) store.add_entry("Apollo", "2026-07-06", 2.5) store.add_entry("Zephyr", "2026-06-29", 6.0) app = create_web_app(store_path) client = TestClient(app) response = client.get("/?week=2026-W28") assert response.status_code == 200 body = response.text assert "Apollo" in body zephyr_row = re.search(r'.*?Zephyr.*?', body, re.S) assert zephyr_row is None total_match = re.search(r'class="ts-total"[^>]*>([^<]*)<', body) assert total_match is not None assert "2.5" in total_match.group(1) def test_filter_by_project_prefills_input(tmp_path): store_path = tmp_path / "store.json" store = TimeStore(store_path) store.add_entry("Apollo", "2026-07-06", 2.5) store.add_entry("Zephyr", "2026-06-29", 6.0) app = create_web_app(store_path) client = TestClient(app) response = client.get("/?project=Zephyr") assert response.status_code == 200 body = response.text assert body.count('data-testid="entry-row"') == 1 assert 'value="Zephyr"' in body def test_combined_filter_with_no_matches_shows_empty(tmp_path): store_path = tmp_path / "store.json" store = TimeStore(store_path) store.add_entry("Apollo", "2026-07-06", 2.5) store.add_entry("Zephyr", "2026-06-29", 6.0) app = create_web_app(store_path) client = TestClient(app) response = client.get("/?week=2026-W27&project=Apollo") assert response.status_code == 200 body = response.text assert "ts-empty" in body assert 'data-testid="entry-row"' not in body def test_malformed_week_returns_422_error_page(tmp_path): client, _ = _make_client(tmp_path) response = client.get("/?week=28") assert response.status_code == 422 body = response.text assert "ts-error" in body error_match = re.search(r'class="ts-error"[^>]*>([^<]*)<', body) assert error_match is not None assert "week" in error_match.group(1) def test_filter_form_present_with_inputs_and_ghost_button(tmp_path): client, _ = _make_client(tmp_path) response = client.get("/") assert response.status_code == 200 body = response.text assert 'data-testid="filter-form"' in body assert re.search(r']*name="week"', body) assert re.search(r']*name="project"', body) assert re.search(r'class="ts-button ts-button--ghost"', body) "}]} ```
